topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The string quartet was playing a particularly saccharine waltz, and I was trying very hard to focus on the intricate pattern of the tablecloth. Then, a familiar silhouette cut through the periphery of my vision. It was him. Mark. He was standing near the champagne fountain, laughing with someone I vaguely recognized. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that momentarily stole the music from my ears. I gripped my glass tighter, my knuckles turning white. A strange lightness washed over my chest, a sudden easing of a tension I hadn't even realized I was carrying. I managed a small, tight smile, turning my attention back to the dance floor, the awkwardness dissolving into a quiet sense of finality.

[story 2]

I was deep in conversation with Sarah about the questionable taste of the open bar when I saw him across the room. He looked… different. More settled, perhaps. My heart gave a sudden, unexpected thump against my ribs, a quick, uneven rhythm. For a second, I froze, my hand halfway to my water glass. Then, I watched him wave—a casual, friendly gesture—and a slow, deep exhale escaped me. The knot that had been tightening in my stomach for weeks simply loosened, unraveling. I let my shoulders drop, the tension draining out of my neck. I offered a small nod, a genuine, unforced expression of acknowledgement, and then deliberately steered my conversation back toward Sarah, my voice sounding steady, almost buoyant.

[story 3]

The noise of the reception was a swirling, overwhelming tide, but when I spotted him near the entryway, everything seemed to sharpen into focus. It was him. The sight of him, standing there amidst the bright lights and forced merriment, triggered an immediate physical shift. A subtle relaxation spread through my entire frame, starting in my jaw and smoothing out across my forehead. I stopped mid-step, just observing. A faint warmth bloomed in my chest, chasing away a lingering chill. I didn't rush toward him; instead, I took a slow, deliberate sip of my drink, letting the quiet acceptance of the moment settle over me. I felt a profound sense of space open up inside, a quiet clearing after a long storm.
